steps.title: Identify what the y-intercept represents
steps.content: The $y$-intercept is the point where a graph crosses the $y$-axis. Every point on the $y$-axis has $x = 0$. Therefore, to find the $y$-intercept, we evaluate $f(0)$.

steps.title: Substitute x = 0 and simplify
steps.content:
$$f(0) = (-8)(2)^0 + 22$$
Since any non-zero number raised to the power of 0 equals 1, we have $2^0 = 1$:
$$f(0) = (-8)(1) + 22 = -8 + 22 = 14$$
So the $y$-intercept is the point $(0, 14)$. That matches choice A — A is correct.
